div.h1: What malloc Actually Does vs Pool Simplicity

p.strong: Why pool allocation is O(1): eliminate the search, eliminate the complexity

text.tspan: Worst Case: O(n) search through free list
Best Case: O(1) if cached, but still 4+ pointer ops
Fragmentation: Splits create tiny unusable blocks
No searching, no splitting, no coalescing
No fragmentation: all blocks identical size

div.p: General-purpose allocator must handle any size request. This flexibility has a cost: pointer chasing through metadata.
Pool allocator knows exact size at init time. No searching, no splitting, no metadata per block.

text.tspan: // 3. Load next pointer from block (1 cache line)
// 3. Load next pointer from block (1 cache line)
